content.problemStatement:
Given an array of integers AAA, and an integer KKK find a number of **happy elements**.

Element XXX is **happy** if there exists at least 111 element whose difference is less than KKK i.e. an element XXX is happy if there is another element in the range [X−K, X+K][X-K,~X+K][X−K, X+K] other than XXX itself.


Input Format
The first line contains two integers NNN and KKK where NNN is the size of the array and KKK is a number as described above. The second line contains NNN integers separated by space.



Output Format
Print a single integer denoting the total number of happy elements.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Other than number 151515, everyone has at least 111 element in the range [X−3,X+3][X-3, X+3][X−3,X+3]. Hence they are all happy elements. Since these five are in number, the output is 555.

Explanation:
All numbers have at least 111 element in the range [X−2, X+2][X-2,~X+2][X−2, X+2]. Hence they are all happy elements. Since these three are in number, the output is 333.
